verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: Challenge to the accusers as to the alleged letter.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: Now I have already called upon God, and His Word, the Only-begotten Son our Lord Jesus Christ, to witness for me, that I have never written to that man, nor received letters from him.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: And as to my accuser, give me leave to ask him a few short questions concerning this charge also.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: How did he come to the knowledge of this matter?

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: Will he say that he has got copies of the letter? for this is what the Arians laboured to prove.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: Now in the first place, even if he can shew writing resembling mine, the thing is not yet certain; for there are forgers, who have often imitated the hand even of you who are Emperors.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: And the resemblance will not prove the genuineness of the letter, unless my customary amanuensis shall testify in its favour.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: I would then again ask my accusers, Who provided you with these copies? and whence were they obtained?

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: I had my writers, and he his servants, who received his letters from the bearers, and gave them into his hand.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: My assistants are forthcoming; vouchsafe to summon the others (for they are most probably still living), and enquire concerning these letters.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: Search into the matter, as though Truth were the partner of your throne.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: She is the defence of Kings, and especially of Christian Kings; with her you will reign most securely, for holy Scripture says, ‘Mercy and truth preserve the king, and they will encircle his throne in righteousness.’

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: And the wise Zorobabel gained a victory over the others by setting forth the power of Truth, and all the people cried out, ‘Great is the truth, and mighty above all things.’
